# Beudox HR - Technical Context for AI Assistants

## Architecture Overview

Beudox HR is a modern React-based HR management system built with TypeScript and Vite. It follows a component-driven architecture with clear separation of concerns:

- **Frontend**: Single-page application with client-side routing
- **Backend**: Supabase (PostgreSQL + Auth + Edge Functions)
- **State Management**: React Query for server state, Context API for authentication
- **UI**: Radix UI primitives with Tailwind CSS for styling
- **Forms**: React Hook Form with Zod schema validation

## Key Architectural Patterns

### Authentication & Authorization
- Supabase Auth handles user sessions and JWT tokens
- Role-based access control with hierarchical permissions (CEO > HR Manager > Team Lead > Employee)
- Protected routes with automatic redirects based on auth state and permissions
- Employee data fetched once on login and cached in AuthContext

### Data Fetching
- React Query for optimistic updates and caching
- Supabase client for database operations
- Real-time subscriptions for live data updates where needed
- Error boundaries and loading states throughout the app

### Component Structure
- Atomic design: UI primitives → Feature components → Page layouts
- shadcn/ui components as the foundation (40+ components)
- Custom business logic components in feature folders
- Layout components (AppLayout, Sidebar, TopBar) for consistent structure

### Routing & Navigation
- React Router DOM with nested routes
- Protected route wrapper with role-based access checks
- Dynamic route parameters for entity IDs (employees, projects, etc.)
- Breadcrumb navigation in top bar

## Database Schema Highlights

The application uses 26+ database migrations defining:

- **Core Entities**: companies, employees, roles, departments
- **HR Features**: evaluations (quarterly/daily), leave_requests, payroll
- **Business**: projects, clients, invoices, expenses
- **Settings**: company settings, HR policies, notification preferences

Key relationships:
- Employees belong to companies and have roles
- Evaluations link employees with reviewers and reviewees
- Projects have employees assigned with roles
- Leave requests have approval workflows

## Key Files and Their Purposes

### Entry Points
- `src/main.tsx`: React root rendering
- `src/App.tsx`: Main app component with routing and providers

### Authentication
- `src/hooks/useAuth.tsx`: Auth context provider managing session state
- `src/pages/Login.tsx`: Authentication UI
- `src/pages/SetPassword.tsx`: Password reset/invite handling

### Layout System
- `src/components/layout/AppLayout.tsx`: Main layout wrapper
- `src/components/layout/AppSidebar.tsx`: Collapsible navigation sidebar
- `src/components/layout/TopBar.tsx`: Page header with breadcrumbs

### Core Components
- `src/components/BeudoxLogo.tsx`: Logo component with variants
- `src/components/SearchableEmployeeSelect.tsx`: Employee selection dropdown
- `src/components/evaluations/EvaluationTimeline.tsx`: Evaluation history display
- `src/components/hr-policies/RichTextEditor.tsx`: WYSIWYG editor for policies

### Business Logic
- `src/lib/role-access.ts`: Permission checking utilities
- `src/lib/leave-utils.ts`: Leave balance calculations
- `src/lib/notifications.ts`: Notification system helpers
- `src/lib/format-date.ts`: Date formatting utilities

### Integrations
- `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts`: Supabase client configuration
- `src/integrations/supabase/types.ts`: Generated TypeScript types

## Development Workflow

- **Build Tool**: Vite with SWC for fast development
- **Testing**: Vitest for unit tests, Playwright for E2E
- **Linting**: ESLint with TypeScript rules
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS with custom design tokens
- **Package Manager**: npm/bun support

## Performance Considerations

- Code splitting with dynamic imports for pages
- Image optimization with lazy loading
- React Query caching to minimize API calls
- Tree shaking with Vite build process
- Bundle analysis available via build tools

## Security

- Row Level Security (RLS) policies in Supabase
- JWT-based authentication
- Input validation with Zod schemas
- XSS protection via React's built-in escaping
- CSRF protection through Supabase's auth system

## Deployment

- Static site deployment to any CDN
- Environment variables for different stages
- Supabase project per environment
- CI/CD ready with npm scripts

## Common Patterns

- Custom hooks for data fetching (useQuery)
- Form components with react-hook-form
- Modal/dialog patterns with Radix UI
- Toast notifications with Sonner
- Responsive design with Tailwind breakpoints
- Dark/light theme support (next-themes)

## File Naming Conventions

- Components: PascalCase (BeudoxLogo.tsx)
- Hooks: camelCase with 'use' prefix (useAuth.tsx)
- Utilities: kebab-case (role-access.ts)
- Pages: PascalCase matching route names (Dashboard.tsx)
- Types: PascalCase interfaces, camelCase types
